reviews.review_description: This is my new revew so ye the game is great umm just needs more things in the future

It would be nice to see a hard mode in this game or randomly generated rooms because this game is difficult but when you complete a level for the 5th time you remember where all the enemies are. And  improved enemy mechanics to make the game more difficult

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review!
It is great to hear the core difficulty is working. The point about memorization is valid, and the request for randomization and evolved enemy mechanics is spot on.
Your feedback is exactly what helps shape where the combat goes next...

Appreciate the support! Thank you

reviews.review_description: This is a great game for those looking for high quality low budget games. It reminds me of the movie edge of tomorrow because when you die you come back and try again and everything is exactly the same so you have to try to strategize and work your way through the levels. The upgrades were perfectly planned as I unlocked everything right as I finished the game. Truly a fun game at an amazing price.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing review!

The comparison to Edge of Tomorrow is a massive compliment. That specific loop of learning through repetition and strategy is exactly what the game aims to capture.

It is also incredibly satisfying to hear that the progression system felt balanced right until the end. Knowing the timing of the unlocks landed perfectly is invaluable feedback.

Appreciate the support!

reviews.review_description:
In the beginning, I really enjoyed the game but the longer I played, the more frustrating some of the elements became. Each level has an unchanging layout including the “enemy” so every time you play the level, they remain standing in their corner just waiting for you. The most annoying part is that there are levels where the enemy is down a long dark corridor so you really can barely see them but they can see you. So with your limited ammo, you’re guessing and shooting down a dark corridor trying to hit a target you can’t clearly see. What makes it worse is that the AI doesn’t miss.  You can peek around a corner trying to shoot but the AI will hit you time and again. There is no indication they’re shooting at you because you don’t see their shots other than when your health is dropping. In what world does AI enemy hit you with such accuracy?  Also, the upgrades are awkward in that you try to turn them on but end up grabbing things or dropping things out of your inventory. The actions should be triggered by physical buttons on the controller not tapping your other hand.  This game has potential which is ruined by the weird AI.  Also, like the other review mentioned, you can see AI through glass windows but they act like you don’t exist. It’s bizarre because they can spot you down a long black hallway and hit you every time but through a glass window, you don’t exist 2 feet away. AI is dumb, so if you trigger them and just go around the corner, they walk right around the corner and you can headshot them every time unless they are down a long dark corridor in which case good luck hitting them before they kill your. The upgrades are mostly useless other than health regeneration since the time warp is so short by the time you’re aiming down the long corridor , AI is shooting again. Lastly, if you run out of ammo, the combat defaults to pushing them onto the ground which is lame.

One other big oversight. The sub machine gun has no sights. It’s just a flat picatinny rail. Like seriously? No iron sights, no red dot.  Just a good old flat picatinny rail.

A fun, well made, no frills shooter. It's one of those games that just works. A must buy for those wanting shorter play sessions but still want to let of some Steam 
Buy it

developer_response.body:
Thanks a ton for the recommendation!
It's awesome to hear the no frills style hit the spot. Creating a focused experience that fits perfectly into those shorter sessions was exactly the goal.

reviews.review_description: Corridor is an original VR experience that starts like an action shooter but quickly reveals itself as a stealth game. The atmosphere is perfectly crafted, with lighting and shadows playing a crucial role as your single torch becomes both a guide and a tactical tool. Moving quietly, taking down enemies, and navigating the dark corridors feels immersive and tense. Backed by solid execution, Corridor is a well-rounded title and a great addition to any VR gamer’s library.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this fantastic review! It’s incredibly rewarding to hear you describe Corridor as an "original VR experience."

You've perfectly captured the game's essence. How it starts with action but quickly reveals its stealthy, tactical core. I'm thrilled that the atmosphere resonated with you and that you found the experience "immersive and tense." Hearing that Corridor is a "well-rounded title" and a "great addition to any VR gamer’s library" is a huge motivator.

Thank you again for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: It’s ok worth the price

An incredibly intense gaming experience that's unforgiving and varies greatly in difficulty. The name of the game says it all; you work your way through corridors full of enemies that demand quick and effective elimination before they take you down. Every corner becomes a test of endurance, and you're constantly hoping for enough ammo. Unfortunately, there are only a few missions, but you'll enjoy replaying them, and with the upgrades and improvements you've earned, a second playthrough is completely different. It's worth every cent and is frequently offered for under $6.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the brilliant review!

It's awesome to read your take on the game. That feeling of every corner being a 'test of endurance' is exactly the kind of intense, rewarding challenge I love in games, and I'm thrilled it resonated with you.  

One always hopes the ideas will connect with players when designing a game.  Reading your words makes it feel like they truly have.

Appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: I only played a few times right about now, but i really feel what the game is going for and i absolutely love pushing through all of these levels. But id wanna know two things. First if theres gonna be more enemy types and challenges to overcome with those enemies? Second if there's gonna be some kind of trippy story to go along with all these environments?

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the great review! It's awesome to hear you're loving the experience and have connected with the core gameplay so quickly.

To answer your questions:
The simulation is always evolving. Introducing new threats and more complex combat puzzles is a key part of that evolution, wherever it may lead.
As for the story: You are a combat robot thrust into a brutal simulation called Corridor, a relentless training ground that fine-tunes your tactical instincts and combat awareness. Beat it, and you prove you're ready for the real fight beyond.

Enjoy the simulation... this and whatever will come next.

reviews.review_description:
Got the game on sale thinking it looked alright for a budget title, and while it's definitely not a big budget game this game is surprisingly good! Love the tension of having to make every bullet count and hoping the next room will have some rounds in it. 

I hope the developer continues to work on this as it's already awesome but some new enemy types and maybe more gun types would be great. I also wish cabinets and drawers were openable to hide pickups. I did find a toilet that was openable tho but nothing else I've tried opens. 

Anyway great game highly recommend 👌

Edit - I just finished the game and WOW those last 2 missions are tough but so exilerating when you finally make it.


Just seen the new update, really like the updated Hub. It's mainly just cosmetic but a nice touch. Really hoping the added languages bring more players so we can get more levels even if it has to be paid DLC :)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the great review, and especially for taking the time to update it with your thoughts after finishing the game and seeing the latest update.

I'm really glad you enjoyed the core gameplay and that the final levels provided a satisfying challenge.

It's incredibly motivating to know you're so invested in what comes next.  Let's just say your desire for more has been heard!

Thanks again for your amazing support and recommendation!

reviews.review_title: Great game if you don't expert much
reviews.review_description:
It's a FPS Rogue like so if you like those 2 you'll love it. Gunplay feels natural, bullet refill seems logical but I never saw that anywhere else. Level design is fine, some secret areas exists. You could sneak onto enemies but once they allert they will rush you. 
Cons: upon starting the game first you see is a tutorial and there are no clear indication where the actual start is. 
Game lacks Valve's environment storytelling. If that room had another doors or elevator textures I would not get lost. Instead I had to bang my head into the wall in order to load the first level. And on the level itself there are no door or ladder or anything telling how I got here.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. We are genuinely glad to hear that you enjoyed the gunplay, core mechanics, and level design, and that the rogue-like FPS blend resonated with you.
Regarding the con you mentioned. You are absolutely right. The lack of clear visual guidance at the very beginning and the insufficient environmental cues are valid points, and they are fully acknowledged. The onboarding and initial navigation should be clearer. This is valuable feedback and highlights an area that deserves improvement.
Reviews like yours are extremely helpful, as they point out not only what works well but also where the experience can be made more intuitive and polished. Thank you again for playing and for sharing your honest perspective. It genuinely helps shape the future of the game.

reviews.review_description: Could not get past tutorial

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for giving the game a try, and I’m really sorry your experience stopped at the tutorial.

Just to clarify: the tutorial isn’t bugged, but the last two tutorial steps only unlock once your avatar level increases. The tutorial room is completely optional – it’s just a demonstration space. To progress in the game:

Go through the wide door opposite the training range, next to the Upgrade terminal – that’s the entrance to the Challenge realm.

In the realms, collect Morphit cells.

When you return to the hub after winning or dying, use the Upgrade terminal to level up your avatar.

At level 5, the next tutorial step unlocks.
At level 7, the final tutorial step unlocks.

Refunds are handled entirely by Meta’s platform, so we don’t have any access or control over them on our end.

Give it another try.

reviews.review_title: Great once you realise the intent
reviews.review_description:
I initially treated this like a typical shooter where you run around blasting away, die then respawn.  This made for a frustrating game with not enough cover or ammo. 

When I went back to it a few hours later with the mindset of "how would you approach this if it was real", so gun raised as I edged around corners, the game clicked into place. It was far more enjoyable, intense and I soon completed a corridor. Love it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this fantastic review! It's awesome to hear how the game "clicked into place" for you.

You’ve perfectly described the experience I was hoping to create. That moment when you shift from a run-and-gun mindset to a more tactical, "how would you approach this if it was real" approach is exactly where the game comes alive. It's designed to be a tense, rewarding challenge, and it's incredibly gratifying for me to hear that you found that enjoyable and intense experience.

Thanks again for giving it a second look and for sharing your journey with it. Happy gaming!

reviews.review_description: When will we get more levels more upgrades I haven't sent an update for this game and decades besides the language update which is not too much king to me need more stages more levels different weapons and we able to change the color of our bot

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the review and for your enthusiasm for the game! It’s fantastic to hear you're enjoying the experience and are eager for more.

Your passion for a bigger world with more weapons, maps, and upgrades is the best kind of feedback a developer can get. It's that kind of vision that's truly inspiring.

Rest assured, the journey doesn't end here. The ideas you’ve mentioned are definitely shaping what the future looks like, perhaps even in places beyond these familiar corridors...

Thanks again for your incredible support!

reviews.review_description: The gameplay is very immersive and it really gets challenging near the end. It's heart pumping ensuring you make every shot count! It took me around 4 hours to beat all 12 levels. I want more levels haha it was soo good!!

developer_response.body:
A heartfelt thank you for this incredible review. Hearing that you want more is the best motivation there is.
You can be sure more is coming, and it's being built on a new foundation.

reviews.review_description:
A smart shooter.
Take your time 
Limited bullets.
Stealth approach. So much fun. Found myself picking up one bullet which saved me at the end. Do not overuse the upgrade so the stress remains intact. 
I am having fun…
Thank you

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for the review.

I absolutely loved your story about picking up a single bullet that saved you.

Your review is incredibly valuable. It truly helps other players who are looking for a real challenge discover the Corridor.

I'm thrilled you're having fun with it. Thank you again!

reviews.review_description: Even though ive %100 this game I still come back to it because it's so good. Almost like a futuristic gunman contracts vibe but on standalone. Hope this game gets more levels or a sequel soon. So good and underrated and underappreciated.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible review!

It is honestly one of the best compliments a developer can receive to hear that you've completed 100% of the game and still come back to it just because you enjoy the gameplay. That is absolutely amazing to hear.

I love the "futuristic gunman contracts" (🤌✨) vibe comparison! Capturing that kind of intense, satisfying loop was a huge goal. Your enthusiasm for more levels or even a sequel is incredibly motivating and the best kind of feedback.

Thank you for being a champion for the game. It's passionate players like you who help underrated games get the appreciation. Your support means the world.

reviews.review_title: Picked it up on sale and I am hooked.
reviews.review_description:
This game is addictive. And although repetitive I like mastering the different levels and making it to the end and the upgrade system is also great. I am not one for fancy words But this game is well balanced and actually very good. Glad I finally decided to give it a shot. And with the 30% off it was a bargain. I swear it was already discounted as well. Cheap and cheerful as we say in UK. 
Oh and I love the headshots and seeing them go barrelling backwards from the impact. Very good.

Definitely a hidden gem. Responsive, accurate and satisfying gunplay, innovative inventory system with two slots on each forearm, and great level design that increases in difficulty as you progress. There is a learning curve, but practice does pay off in this game.

Although this isn't a horror game, sneaking around the often dark and claustrophobic hallways can really get your heart rate up. When aggressive enemies spot you and start running at you, the fear-factor and adrenaline can really ramp up, which is awesome. In these moments, the Slow-mo ability is a life-saver, providing precious extra seconds to make sure your dwindling bullets find an enemy's skull.

The skill upgrade system works really well, and upgrading your abilities like Sprint and Slow-mo feels like a fair reward after slogging through the challenging levels and finding currency hidden throughout the environments. When you eventually max out your stats and ablilities, you do feel a lot more powerful and prepared when going back into the levels, but you're definitely not invincible. Over-confidence and complacency can be devastating, and if you're not focusing, the game's enemies can really catch you off-guard.

Corridor is somewhat held back by only having 2 guns - a pistol and an SMG. A one-handed sawn-off shotgun or maybe a single-shot pistol with a red dot sight or scope could really add some extra value to the experience. However, considering that this is (I believe) a single-developer game, the two available guns are more than precise and powerful enough to get the job done.

The mechanic for grabbing items from a distance can feel a little tricky at first and takes a bit of practice, but it does eventually become second-nature.

It's worth mentioning that the video trailer for this game is not representative of the actual gameplay, at least in my experience - you're not going to be sprinting around, spraying endless bullets and switching between guns all the time. Instead, you're far more likely to be moving tactically through corridors, peeking around corners, conserving ammo and picking your shots carefully. Accuracy matters in this game. If you keep missing your shots, you WILL run out of ammo, and at that point... you're kinda screwed. I saw one reviewer mention that there "isn't enough ammo", but this just isn't true - as long as you're shooting accurately, searching carefully for magazines and bullets, and managing your inventory effectively, there's enough ammo to survive and complete each level. When you're down to your last couple of bullets, searching around and finding a full magazine can be a massive relief! Even finding 2 or 3 bullets on a shelf can mean the difference between failure and success, which can make encounters with enemies feel high-stakes and intense.

I know there are other games that "make you feel like John Wick", but when you start getting good at Corridor, consistently hitting those headshots, reloading smoothly and staying aware of exactly how many bullets you have left, the game really finds its stride and becomes ridiculously fun.

Potential improvements:
A) It would be great if enemies would occasionally crouch, duck and sidestep to evade bullets, or sometimes find cover and lean around corners rather than always charging around them - after playing for a while the enemy behavior becomes very predictable (enemies either stay fixed on the spot and snipe at you from a distance, or they charge towards you). A few extra animations would really alleviate this and add another layer of challenge. Even having variation in enemy heights would help, as you'd have to adjust your aim for each enemy to get headshots, rather than consistently aiming at the same height when enemies run around corners towards you. Having a few taller opponents could also increase their intimidation factor.

B) Triggering the Sprint and Slow-mo abilities using buttons on the back of your in-game hands can be fiddly and awkward. Having a button activation (e.g. Y/B/left stick button/right stick button) for the Sprint and Slow-mo abilities would make them quicker and more intuitive to use when the bullets start flying.

C) New Game Plus mode, where you could replay each level with a lot more ammo available (possibly starting with 2 pistols, or 2 SMGs, for dual wielding), lots more enemies, and random enemy spawn locations each time you load into a level, rather than fixed placement of enemies. This would be an awesome reward for completing Corridor, and would hugely increase the replay value.

Having played each of the 12 levels a few times, Corridor has become a great go-to game for when I have 20 minutes to kill, and want a quick challenge or to brush-up on my reflexes and aiming. Playing Corridor has improved my pistol aim in other games, just due to Corridor's necessity to make every bullet count!

I highly recommend this game, it's obviously not made on a big budget by a huge development team, and there's so much potential in this game that could be brought out with a few tweaks and updates, but it still succeeds in providing a really solid and polished experience for fans of shooters and those seeking a challenge. Well worth the £12 price tag, but if it's on sale, definitely don't hesitate.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this absolutely incredible and insightful review. It is genuinely moving. Your time and effort in writing something so thoughtful, detailed, and passionate are deeply appreciated.

Reading your words was truly inspiring. This is not only because of the careful description of your experience with the game, but also because so many of the highlighted aspects are the exact ones that received the most focus during development. Seeing those efforts resonate so strongly with a player is deeply rewarding.

Your description of the gameplay experience is spot on. The tension, adrenaline, and tactical movement articulated the exact feeling the game aimed to evoke. While not a horror game, creating those heart-pounding moments when enemies charge and you’re fumbling for your last few bullets is a core part of the design. The Slow-mo ability is indeed a lifesaver, and it’s rewarding to hear that the upgrade system feels fair and empowering without making you feel invincible. That balance was a key goal.
It’s also great that you touched on the difference between the trailer and the actual gameplay. You've nailed it - this is a game about tactical precision, not running-and-gunning. It’s a challenge of conservation and accuracy, and the relief of finding a single magazine when running on empty is a central part of the experience. Thank you for highlighting that so clearly. Hearing that the game "finds its stride and becomes ridiculously fun" when you get good at it is everything a developer could hope for a player to discover.

I also want to thank you for your incredibly constructive feedback. Your potential improvements are not just valid; they are brilliant and align perfectly with where I'd love to take the game.

Thank you again for this phenomenal, comprehensive, and insightful review. It's not just feedback; it's a huge source of motivation and inspiration. Knowing that playing Corridor has even improved your aim in other games is perhaps the coolest compliment possible! Thanks for being a part of this journey.

reviews.review_description: Log on, do a few runs, log off. There’s no real story just pure breach-like combat that’s actually challenging

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the spot-on review!

Happy to hear you're enjoying the pure, challenging combat runs. You've perfectly captured the heart of the game!

reviews.review_description:
I'm starting to get into the game, it's good fun.
Why can't you put sprint on the joystick?? Perfectly normal controls!!.
4 inventory slots on ur arms sooo inconvenient!
 No fast reload, I love the mechanic of loading one bullet at a time into empty cartridges but COME ON!! you needed to rethink the controllers for all this.
Would be a solid game. Still enjoyed it tho keep going devs.. If you are still working on it idk.
Good shooting fun

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback, I'm glad you're enjoying the game.

I appreciate you sharing your specific thoughts on the controls and mechanics. Many of these systems, like the arm-based inventory and the deliberate reloading, were designed to be different from standard VR shooter conventions to create a more methodical and high-stakes tactical feel. The goal is to make every action feel very intentional. I understand this approach can feel challenging at first, but it's at the heart of the experience. As players progress and the challenge ramps up, mastering these systems often becomes a crucial and rewarding part of the strategy.

Thanks for taking the time to share your perspective.

reviews.review_description: After the developer clarified that the non -intuitive training mode has bizarre level requirements that no other game I have ever played has.. . . I've bumped my review to 3 stars. Will update again after I've taken the time to play it more, but I've already moved on to other shooting games, because this one seems buggy with the 'grabbing' and aiming and irritated the piss out of me. Two other shooting games that I purchased at the same time can be 'immediately' played with no counter-intuitive "training" & bugs.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Sorry for the frustration - nothing should feel “stuck,” so let me clarify how the training steps unlock.

In the Training Room, only Steps 6 (Sprint) and 7 (Time Warp) start locked. These are not unlocked by repeating the tutorial - they unlock automatically when your avatar level increases.

To level up your avatar, you just need to:
1. Play levels, collect Morphit cells, and
2. Spend the Morphit cells to level up your avatar in using the upgrade terminal.

Sprint unlocks at Avatar Level 5
Time Warp unlocks at Avatar Level 7
Once you reach those levels, both steps will unlock normally.

reviews.review_description:
No brightness setting in game, a vsomewhat discombobulated way of arranging inventory, and why on earth is movement speed a grind upgrade?
Despite plenty of positive reviews, I don't feel that positive from my own experience.
The darkness in graphics update shouldn't be a thing forced upon users, but made possible through in-game settings.
Initial movement speed needs increasing to a more acceptable level, and if we can't have a brightness setting can we at least have a brighter 'headlight' so we can see the bad guy at the end of the cellar from the first corner or the guy coming out of the archway in hospital.
I'm not a fan of grinding this much.

This isn't the first game where someone has said it's too dark where's a brightness setting and the dev/s said "that's the way the game is supposed to be".

This isn't conducive for physically healthy gameplay and good eyesight, I imagine it only worse for older gamers, it's worse still when the screenshots and/or gameplay mislead purchasers into buying something that isn't of the portrayed quality. I bought this because the videos of gameplay implied  that it would still be relatively comfortable on the eye to play, and it hasn't been.

Whilst a dev is well within their creative license and rights to adjust a games features and/or visual elements, it should not come at a physical cost in discomfort to any players.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback on the game.
The lighting and progression systems are core to the intended experience. The darker atmosphere is designed to build tension, while the upgrades, including movement speed, are meant to provide a tangible sense of character growth and mastery over time.
We are continuously monitoring the overall game balance based on how players interact with these core mechanics. Your perspective on the initial difficulty and grind has been noted. We appreciate you taking the time to detail your experience.

reviews.review_description:
The game is not bad but from what I saw is too basic and lacks certain things that would make it complete and more engaging. First thing, the enemies and objects are always in the same points. The enemies behave all in the same way. There is no background music if not always the same when the enemies are on alert. There are no explosive barrels, unlockable doors, safety video cameras and such stuff that would add gameplay. The sounds are good but as far as the player is concerned, it would be cool to put the sounds of servomotors (robotics) for the movement of arms joints, hands and fingers and legs (even if they are not seen) in. The graphics are not bad and the robotic arms of the player are made quite well, as well as weapons, enemies and surrounding objects but lack of destructible stuff and shadowing. In any case, a discreet gameplay  and good for that price (discounted).

Update: I completed much more difficult games than this.

Update 2: I have completed the game and now I can give a fully objective judgment. By removing certain things I mentioned in the first impression and that can be personal tastes, the game has bugs that will lead you to fail over and over again. First of all I found bizarre that the enemies do not see you through the transparent glasses (specifically wanted by the developer), you don't notice your light (specifically wanted by the developer), but they see you through the walls (and this It is not want to developer but it is a programming error), the bullets strike you through solid objects (and this did not want to developer but a programming error. I also happened to me the arms of the avatar left for their business, Just in the most crucial moments, taking to failure. I found the trigger system of special abilities, which resulting too close to the spare loaders, they are to compromise your choice (you want to activate the skill but makes you lose the charger leaving it to fall on the ground, or vice versa you want to take the charger but activate the skill). Unlike the philosophy of the game (where the enemies should always be in the same points) I found enemies out of place and sometimes it seemed to me that the CPU is invented of his own. There is no history and enemies are only three types (those who remain still, those who move and those who remain still and move if they find out, all almost aesthetically equal, changing only the colors, masks and weapons supplied. For the rest the game is not bad, even if I continue to think that with the additions mentioned in the first impression the game would have been more engaging and even adjusting the aforementioned errors developer(s) it could also be allowed to lift the sale price. I recommend it with caution because the game is very much taxative about saving bullets (very counted), the enemies are many and hit the enemy in the head is not always easy. Beyond this the game is not what it seems in the trailer but it is much more "than calm and cold blood" and you will only like it if you are endowed with that Quality.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your honest and detailed feedback. I genuinely appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts on the game's design.

You've touched on some of the core pillars of the game's philosophy. It was intentionally designed to be a hardcore challenge centered on mastery and repetition. The fixed positions of enemies and items transform each corridor into a complex, high-stakes puzzle. The goal isn't to create a dynamic, unpredictable world, but rather a gauntlet that tests your precision, memory, and strategic execution, pushing you to perfect your run with every attempt. It's a game about feeling the satisfaction of flawlessly clearing a level that once felt impossible.

I completely understand that this specific type of challenge, which rewards memorization and methodical improvement over emergent gameplay, isn't for everyone. Your desire for more environmental interaction, like explosive barrels and dynamic elements, is totally valid and speaks to a different, equally enjoyable kind of shooter experience.

Your review is incredibly valuable because it helps clarify what makes the game resonate with its intended audience. Thank you again for giving it a shot and for providing such a thoughtful perspective.

reviews.review_description: In this game you run 12 different corridors, that unlock in 4 different chunks. You unlock 2 abilities and a bunch of stat boost from picking up items. There are 3 enemy types, 2 aggressive types and one sharpshooter that never moves, ever. Every time you enter a corridor its exactly the same, bullets are in the same spot, and enemies start it the same spot. There are 2 weapons the entire game. You get to know your weapons really well by the end of the game. It’s really challenging but I felt the need to try and beat it. You really have to conserve ammo which means getting mostly headshots on most the levels. As some of the levels are really cool looking, even though nothing really makes sense. Why am i a robot fighting these soldiers? Not sure. Why am i fighting though these abandoned building? who knows. It was fun though.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this honest review. It really captures the core of the experience we aimed to build. The challenge and repetition were designed to test mastery, so it’s incredibly rewarding to hear that it drove you to keep going. I'm glad the ammo conservation and headshot-focused combat stood out. Those elements were carefully tuned to push precision and planning.

As for the narrative oddities, fair point! There's definitely a surreal layer to it all. Thanks again for taking the time to write this. Your feedback means a lot and helps the game evolve.

reviews.review_description: This is a great game,but sometimes reaching out to grab items just not work…no matter how I move my arms around cannot not grab items,,other then that this is a great game worth every penny,please try and fix this problem

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback and positive comments on the game.
Your experience with the item grabbing is noted! Our goal is always to ensure a smooth and responsive experience for every player.
I appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: 2 star

Original review 6/15/25
What’s with the super slow walking speed? It makes it a pain in the butt to explore the levels in any way… I’ll rate this 5 stars as soon as that’s fixed or an option is added to add sprint.  It’s a shame cuz the gunplay is actually incredibly satisfying. Some of the best I’ve experienced in vr. The pistol sound/recoil is GREAT. 
One minor complaint… it’s tough to pick up certain items… like when there’s 5-6 bullets laying around + a mag or 2, it’s difficult to highlight the exact item I want. Many times I’m aiming to pick a bullet up but it highlights the clip instead… 

I realize there’s an upgrade for increased walking speed… Ive upgraded 3 times already and it’s still pretty dang slow. I’m hoping once that ability is maxed out it’ll be a decent speed.  If so, I’ll update my rating to 5 stars.


UPDATE 6/19/25
As promised, I’ve changed this to 5 stars. The slow movement was my only big gripe. Thanks for the quick update

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for updating your review and for the honest feedback! We’re really glad the movement speed fix hit the mark - it was a great suggestion, and we appreciate you sticking with it.

Awesome to hear you’re enjoying the gunplay, especially the pistol feel and recoil. That’s exactly the kind of experience we aimed to deliver.

We’re actively working on improving item interactions, especially when multiple objects are close together. Thanks again for the support and for helping make the game better!

reviews.review_title: Not sure if I should enjoy?
reviews.review_description: As someone who has beat zero caliber 1 and 2 multiple times each, I can't help but compare it some. I've barely played the 1st realm before getting kinda miffed on how little I can do. The "training" doesn't feel complete and it took me longer than I wanted to even start playing because you can't run? And the entryway is in the "vault" apparently while the sign just says "enter through the wide doors." Like I recall someone else saying, maybe storing the guns somewhere else would be better than the arms. I also ran out of ammo and yet couldn't seem to melee kill anyone? I kept punching repeatedly and he would go down and get back up (the black robot guy). I imagine you run out of bullets a lot so hand-to-hand combat will be necessary it seems like. After the guy wouldn't die and shot me to death, I just kind of got annoyed and exited the app. Am missing something I should know to improve my experience?

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for taking the time to share your impressions. Your feedback struck a chord. Especially the part about the enemy not dying after repeated melee hits.

Since your session, Corridor has been updated: enemies now take real damage when knocked down and will die if hit again. It makes close-quarters combat far more satisfying and viable when ammo runs low. We genuinely hope you give it another go. It sounds like you were just on the verge of discovering what makes the game click.

While Zero Caliber and Corridor might seem similar on the surface, they approach gameplay quite differently. Corridor is built around tight, tactical challenges with limited resources and no hand-holding. Every ability and shortcut is something you unlock or earn. Sprinting, movement boosts, even combat-enhancing upgrades all unfold as part of your progression.

We know it's not for everyone at first glance, but those who lean into its systems often find a uniquely rewarding experience. If you give it another shot, we’d love to hear how it feels now.

reviews.review_description:
This is a hidden gem on the meta quest store. The game is very unique and has a great feel to it. Normally the gun play is off in these indy titles but this one is on point. The weapons, although very few look great also. The silver 1911 style pistol is a welcome choice oppossed to the traditional black glock 9mm... I like that you can't run and gun through the levels. You have to conserve your ammo and hit your shots
It's hard to put into words but this game captures something special, it has a feel to it that has that "it" factor, and with the right lore/story could make this one of those cult classics like portal or shotwood. The trailer doesn't do this game justice in my opinion.
I like the progression/perk system. Especially the time stall and the ability to make it last longer, going into previously finished levels to grab morphit (in game currency to level up) is fun and you need modest, not excessive amounts for these upgrades. I think the balance of this game is very spot on and that's difficult to get just right so I tip my hat to the dev team.
I agree with another reviewer of this game who said it would be cool if the guns came out of your arm more organically, similar to how robocop takes the pistol out of his leg. I think the arms look a little frail too, the avatar reminds me of a battle Droid from the star wars movies, which is cool but I'd like to see it be a little tougher looking, maybe some battle markings and the ability to see my chest plate, not just my arms. Maybe some cosmetic options to customize the look and color scheme of the robot would be great. Attachments for the guns (flashlight, Lazer sight, red dot, etc) would really ice the cake.
With those things mentioned, the game as it stands is wonderful and a blast to play. It's well worth the 15 bucks. I highly recommend this to anyone who likes shooters and doesn't mind a challenge. I hope this game takes off and gets the recognition it deserves.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible review and for calling the game a "hidden gem"! It absolutely makes my day to hear you say it has that special "it" factor. That is something I've poured a lot of heart into capturing.
I'm thrilled you're enjoying the gunplay and that it feels "on point". Getting the weapon feel right was super important. And yes, the silver 1911 is a personal favorite too! It's fantastic that you appreciate the focus on ammo conservation and precision; it's exactly the kind of challenging, tactical experience I aimed for. Hearing that the progression and upgrade balance feels "spot on" is also incredibly rewarding, as that's a tough thing to nail.
Thanks for the awesome suggestions too! The RoboCop-style weapon emergence is a cool visual, definitely food for thought! I also hear you on wanting a tougher-looking avatar with more customization and potential weapon attachments. It's feedback like this that really helps shape the future.

Seriously, thank you for taking the time to share such detailed thoughts and for recommending the game. Knowing it's providing a "wonderful" and challenging experience means everything. Your support and belief that it deserves more recognition is hugely motivating. Stay tuned, and thanks again for being part of the journey!

reviews.review_description: Most games make you feel like some big shot hunter mowing down enemies in this game I feel like I'm actually having to survive and it is absolutely a way more grounded take that I love when it comes to gunfights and stealth my only complaint is that the sprint and time warp abilities are way too easy to accidentally activate in my experience which is really annoying especially with the small charge time warp starts out with being wasted other than that 10/10 experience

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this incredible review!
You've absolutely nailed the core feeling targeted. Your distinction between feeling like a "hunter" versus "having to survive" is a perfect description of the experience. I'm thrilled that this more grounded, high-stakes approach to gunfights resonated with you so strongly.
Thank you also for the specific and very valuable feedback on the Sprint and Time Warp activation. I completely understand how accidentally triggering those abilities can break the tension. That's a crucial point of user experience, and your feedback is incredibly helpful and is heard!
Thank you again for taking the time to share such a thoughtful and constructive review. It's feedback like yours that truly helps the game evolve.

reviews.review_description: At first I thought the game was another early access ￼dud purchase, glitches and etc. I was completely wrong about this one. I read a lot of reviews most  so so but I figured give it a chance and Oh my goodness this is one bad a$$ game I hope the developers keep updating new features to this game the time ￼ warp is awesome. I have completed 6 levels and the game gets better and better great job on this ,would be nice if you can grap a enemy and use them as a shield this could be the next generation splinter cell kudos developer well worth the money

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving the game a chance and sharing your fantastic review! I'm thrilled that it exceeded your expectations and you're enjoying the Time Warp. It's definitely one of my favorite mechanics too. Grabbing enemies as shields is indeed an awesome idea. Let's just say it's very much on my mind for future developments! Your comparison to Splinter Cell made my day and the next few weeks! Thanks again for the support and the great suggestion. Stay tuned for exciting things ahead!

reviews.review_description: While I'm still not (madly) in love with the game (can't stand that loose bullet thing) this developer listens to complaints/concerns and does something about it in pretty close to real time, which is kind of refreshing although, I'm sure, with time, the industry will beat that out of him/her. Also, my daughter doesn't like that it doesn't have an easy reload option but, she's a kid with no money so she doesn't count PLUS, it gave me the opportunity to tell her "Life doesn't come with easy reload, princess", which was fun.
It’s a game with too many enemies and not enough ammo. The dev says it’s by design but bad design is what all bad games share. I’ve played enough to know them when I see them.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. Game balance is something we continuously evaluate based on the broader player base. Sounds like you might be on an older version - try updating and giving it another shot.

reviews.review_description: This game is hidden gem

developer_response.body: Thrilled to hear you're enjoying the polished feel and see it as a hidden gem! Your words truly inspire me to keep pushing the game further. Glad you're enjoying it!

reviews.review_description:
Corridor embodies a rigorous approach to the VR shooter.
A tight framework and enemies to be precisely eliminated, leaving no room for improvisation.
The Time Warp (temporal slowdown) and Sprint systems allow for precise rhythm management, ideal for preparing each shot.
The VR actions (picking up, loading, and reloading ammunition) are realistically recreated.
Progression via upgrades adds a relevant strategic dimension.
The increasing difficulty forces players to optimize each round and target weak points—a real workout for developing their reflexes.
The gameplay remains comfortable despite the intensity. The game is fluid and beautiful.
Corridor aims for excellence in its category, and it achieves it.
Without unnecessary modes or narrative distractions, it offers a performance-oriented journey.
A must-have for fans of demanding VR shooting.
My video review in french: https://youtu.be/w2FfeKmlRWM

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you for this absolutely outstanding and insightful review!
You have masterfully captured the very essence of what Corridor aims to be. Reading your words:  "performance-oriented journey" that "aims for excellence in its category"  is incredibly rewarding and motivating.
Your video review is just as masterfully done, perfectly illustrating the high-skill, rhythmic gameplay.
Thank you again for your incredible support and for creating such high-quality, thoughtful content. It truly means the world.

reviews.review_description: This game is super Bad A$$, the guns feel great and the accuracy is spot on.  The graphics are nice close up and exactly what they “need” to be for the game.  Gameplay is awesome, the stealth aspect is really cool and the enemies are smart and I’m assuming even smarter and more unpredictable at higher difficulties.  The upgrade system is intuitive and really make the game shine with time warp, healing capabilities, and reliance rush, sprint you can bring some well deserved pain in a stylish way to the enemies!!  This is a really cool game and should be mentioned with top shooters on the metaquest.  It is a new, fresh addition to the meta library and does things differently in a genius way that just works.  Way to go team!! Please keep the content, new guns, new upgrades and new maps coming.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your amazing review! 💎 It means a lot to hear you’re enjoying the game. I appreciate the feedback and your interest in more content - stay tuned! 🙌🔥

reviews.review_description: This game has brilliant mechanics, can't think of a game on quest with better mechanics,it feels great..really good game and a bit of a challenge..a keeper

developer_response.body: Thank you sincerely, from the bottom of my heart.  Your review is incredibly rewarding. I'm delighted to hear you're enjoying the mechanics and finding the challenge engaging. Your enthusiasm inspires me to keep pushing forward and deepening the gameplay even further. Hope you continue having a great time with it!

reviews.review_title: 5! But Feels Like It’s Missing Something
reviews.review_description:
There’s something incongruent about the player’s futuristic aesthetic vs. the generic enemies/environment. Maybe add cyberpunk details to the latter two for consistency?

AI Suggestions: More behavior variation. It’s too easy to hold a corner while like four guys form a conga line of easy headshots. Having groups behave differently than individuals would also help with immersion. *minor request: voice lines that become increasingly panicked as they watch their friends die, with the last guy trying to retreat? I wanna feel like a terminator lol

Dumb/Fun Suggestion: If it doesn’t mess with gameplay (specifically the balancing of four inventory slots) it would be cool for the weapons to be fold into the arms and mechanically pop out (like DiCaprio’s handshake gun in Django Unchained). 

Has tons of potential, just needs to lean into some of the unique features! It’s like a prepubescent game that’s starting to finds its identity 🙃

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your detailed feedback! I really appreciate the thought you put into this. It’s immensely valuable to hear how you experience the game.
Enemy behavior and environmental cohesion are definitely interesting areas to explore.
And I have to say, the idea of enemies reacting more dynamically to their dwindling numbers is a fun one. Love that “terminator” vibe))
As for weapon design, there’s always room for interesting possibilities. It’s fun to imagine how things could evolve.
Thank you for playing and sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_title: Seems like the difficulty is way off
reviews.review_description: You get a handgun with one magazine that holds like 7 rounds and get swarmed by 3-4 guys at the very start of the level. Who don't drop ammo. So unless you get headshots every time you run out of ammo instantly and there's nothing you can do at that point. And the aiming is not great the sights on the handgun don't work for me and the smg doesn't even have sights.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! Just to clarify, the handgun magazine holds 10 rounds, not 7. Ammo management is part of the challenge, encouraging precise shooting and scavenging for resources. Regarding the SMG, the lack of sights is intentional. Each weapon is designed to excel in different scenarios, so the SMG isn’t always superior to the pistol. That said, we appreciate your thoughts and will keep balancing in mind as we refine the experience!

reviews.review_description: This game is so good! The atmosphere is amazing. The enemy is on point and the guns work great! The upgrade system is spot on. Just a great intense game. Please add more guns 😬

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review! We're thrilled you're enjoying the game. More guns would be exciting - we'll keep that in mind! 🚀

reviews.review_description:
This is a pretty decent VR shooter! 

12 different corridors to complete and master, the game it seems to start off slow on purpose so you can become accustomed to the game and it’s controls. 

The initial training room is very intuitive and well thought through to allow the user to become au fait with the game and its controls.

Once you are through the first few corridors that’s when the game starts to come into its own, and you start to unlock your special abilities and are able to seat upgrading you avatar and player settings

The game feels very intuitive and is very rewarding once you’ve got the hang of this, mid way through the corridors become way more challenging to master, and the environments get even nicer. 

A very well thought out and executed FPS! 

Excited for future updates on this I’m not normally a shooter type player but this has such a good feel about  it that it just keeps drawing me back to play it! 

Well done! Highly recommend it.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your opinion! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the game, especially as a non-shooter player. Your feedback means the world to us, and we’re excited to bring you more updates soon. Thanks for the recommendation - it truly makes our day! 😊

reviews.review_description: Only hope it gets more levels and randomised enemies placement.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the 5-star review! I'm really glad you’re enjoying the gameplay and gun mechanics. I appreciate your feedback and enthusiasm for more content.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely amazing VR experience! This game is the perfect blend of a hardcore shooter and old-school, hard to beat gameplay that makes you WORK for every WIN!!! The challenge is real—you’ll die countless times, but that’s what makes mastering each level so satisfying. The mechanics are tight, the difficulty is perfect, and the sense of progression as you refine your shooting skills is unmatched. I’ve been hooked for hours since buying the game and can’t wait to dive back in. Highly recommend it to anyone who loves a challenge and misses the golden age of tough games!

Corridor is honestly one of the best VR shooters I’ve played! The physics are spot on—everything feels so real, like you’re actually there. Plus, you can try different worlds and spaces, and they’re really well done. It’s super entertaining and keeps you engaged the whole time. If you’re into VR shooters, you’ve gotta try this one. Totally worth it! Can’t wait to see what updates they bring next!
Let's go!!!  I'm so glad I found this Gem!!  This is exactly what I was looking for!  Keep going with the quality, I'm here for all the fun!!  Corridor I say no more!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! 🙌 It means the world to hear that Corridor is exactly what you were looking for. Your support keeps me going, and I can’t wait for you to see what’s next. Enjoy the journey, and thanks for being part of it! 🚀🔥💙

reviews.review_description:
I love this game. My brother constantly recommends random VR shooter experiences and this was the first one I enjoyed fully. I left with a feeling of needing more of it after I completed.
Game started slow as any VR experience, with easy levels to get accustomed to the game then rapidly ramped up difficulty. As a fan of Roguelites this game was excellent. The traps and challenges, while difficult always seemed fair.
I want more of it, in the meantime will try doing some challenge runs without upgrades :)

This game is very fun and exciting. The shooting feels real, and the VR makes it very immersive. The graphics are good, and the sound makes it feel like you are in the game. The controls are easy to learn, even for new players. Some levels are hard, but it makes the game more challenging. Overall, it is a great game for anyone who likes action and shooting.
